prompt: Surrealist fine art photography. Cold silver, deep navy, pale gold light. A grand hall of standing ornate mirrors on a reflective marble floor, shot at a three-quarter angle from low height. The asymmetry is the entire point and must be visible: the nearest mirror reflects a space containing exactly two other mirrors. Inside that reflection, four mirrors are visible. Inside that, eight. The count of mirrors visible doubles with each successive layer of reflection, receding into the distance. The mirror you are closest to is always the most sparsely surrounded. Every mirror you look INTO shows a more densely populated space than the one you are currently at. This must read as a structural fact, not just atmospheric depth. No people. No figures. No text. No numbers. No labels. Pure mirrors, marble, cold light, infinite recession. Mood: vertiginous mathematical certainty, quiet impossibility. Photorealistic, not painterly. Shot as if by a large-format camera. Extremely sharp foreground, soft infinite depth behind.
visual_logic: Each mirror reflects a space containing more mirrors than itself — every vantage point is a periphery. No mirror is the center.  The mathematical fact that every node's neighbors have higher average degree is rendered as infinite spatial recession.
